examples: The robed and bewigged lords of the bench may soon be more of a media fixture than the rough courtroom sketch.
Bewigged tragedy has a reason for its existence, and I am not one of those who, by order of AEschylus, contest its right to existence.
The bewigged officers and ragged sailors add a theatrical element and will help bring the voyage more alive to younger viewers.
Two segments of a late Edo Period scroll now in the Chester Beatty Library in Dublin show bewigged Dutchmen smoking, playing billiards and tending their vegetable patch.
